description: Measures excess kurtosis of each channel window. Gaussian noise has kurtosis ~3; spike-containing windows push kurtosis well above 3. The score is (kurtosis - 3) * rms, combining peakiness with amplitude. A Winsorization step clips the top/bottom 5% of samples before computing kurtosis to prevent single-sample outliers from dominating.
notes: Kurtosis detects non-Gaussian transients regardless of their polarity. Winsorization prevents a single artifact sample from inflating kurtosis. The excess_kurtosis gate provides a second confirmation that the window is genuinely peaky, not just high-amplitude noise.
